extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a can only become true via the rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is (hepatic_impairment AND ckd_dialysis AND immunosuppressed AND heart_failure AND pregnancy). This requires hepatic_impairment = true. However, hepatic_impairment = true also satisfies the antecedent of rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), which sets rec_drug_b := true. Each output (a and b) has exactly one rule that can set it to true; there are no overriding rules, so priorities and tie-breaking are irrelevant, and the default false only applies when an output is left unset. Therefore any input making rec_drug_a true necessarily makes rec_drug_b true, violating mutual exclusion. Concrete counterexample: set the five conjunction conjuncts true (hepatic_impairment, ckd_dialysis, immunosuppressed, heart_failure, pregnancy), with qt_prolongation and elderly false (they only affect rec_drug_c). Then rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a = true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b = true), so (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is true. Property violated.
